name: Chaperone and Heat-Shock Support Coverage Repair

scope.question: Which Chaperone and Heat-Shock Support source-completeness gaps from the June 10 surveillance assessment should be repaired publicly, and does repair change the public evidence stage or boundary?

summary: Coverage repair staged the two high-priority public gaps that materially improve the current evidence map: PMID 40663813 for the NPC-002 48-month open-label extension and the March 2026 DailyMed Miplyffa label for indication, safety, and OCT2 interaction context. The staged bundle creates two sources, updates the arimoclomol NPC study, creates one durability/safety finding and one label-safety boundary finding, and refreshes the track outlook. The proposed public read keeps evidence_stage human_functional_benefit, momentum mixed, and confidence low because the added evidence remains NPC-specific and does not establish broad aging or LEV-relevant proteostasis restoration.

materiality_decision.rationale: The open-label extension and current label materially improve source completeness, durability context, and safety language for the existing NPC-specific public claim. They do not change the stage, momentum, or confidence because the repair does not add independent broad-aging, mammalian aging, or generalized chaperone-support evidence.

search_log.summary: Confirmed PMID 40663813 as the 2025 Molecular Genetics and Metabolism report of NPC-002 48-month open-label extension efficacy and safety. Screened PMID 41567854 as drug-interaction review context, PMID 39715913 as arimoclomol first-approval review context, and PMID 42007957 as NPC therapeutics review context; none of those reviews added a new primary efficacy result.

Confirmed label version 5, effective March 18, 2026 and published March 20, 2026. The label indicates Miplyffa in combination with miglustat for neurological manifestations of NPC in patients age 2 years and older, warns about hypersensitivity, embryofetal toxicity, and creatinine increases, and identifies OCT2 substrate interaction management.

Re-checked the main arimoclomol trials, the ALS and IBM open-label extensions, Gaucher arimoclomol boundary context, NPC early-access context, and the BGP-15 dose-ranging registry. The NPC parent trial remains completed with posted results and actual study completion on 2024-10-31; ALS and IBM parent trials and extensions remain result-bearing null or terminated disease-program context; Gaucher, early-access NPC, and BGP-15 records do not create a public update.

search_log.source_name: Local public records and prior coverage assessment
search_log.query: Chaperone and Heat-Shock Support outlook, source, study, finding, evidence-review, publication-event, and June 10 surveillance coverage assessment
search_log.summary: Compared staged repair candidates against the latest public outlook and the chaperone-and-heat-shock-support-surveillance-2026-06-10 coverage gaps. Only the NPC open-label extension and current label were selected for public staging in this bounded repair.

trial_watch_checks.summary: NPC-002 remains completed with posted results. The repair stages the registry completion dates, the 48-month open-label extension source, a durability/safety finding, and the corresponding outlook support-map refresh.
The ALS phase 3 registry remains completed with posted results and no interpretation change. The existing public finding already captures the null disease-program boundary.
The inclusion body myositis registry remains completed with posted results and no interpretation change. The existing public finding already captures the null disease-program boundary.
Terminated ALS open-label extension with posted results. It remains disease-program boundary context and was not staged because the parent ALS null trial already carries the public limitation.
Terminated inclusion-body-myositis open-label extension with posted results. It remains non-NPC disease-program boundary context and was not staged in this NPC-focused repair.
Terminated Gaucher disease arimoclomol registry with no posted results. It is lysosomal disease boundary context and was not staged as chaperone-support evidence.
Available NPC early-access program with no posted results or completion dates. It was kept as post-approval context rather than staged public evidence.

Terminated BGP-15 type 2 diabetes dose-ranging registry with no posted results and month-level actual completion dates in 2011. The existing public map already includes the published BGP-15 metabolic proof-of-concept paper, so this no-results registry was not staged.

excluded_sources.title: Understanding Pharmacokinetic-Drug Interactions With Drugs Approved by the US Food and Drug Administration in 2024 to Better Manage the Risk of Drug Interactions With Concomitant Medications
excluded_sources.reason: Review-level drug-interaction context was screened, but the DailyMed label is the primary source for the OCT2 interaction language selected for public staging.

excluded_sources.title: Arimoclomol: First Approval
excluded_sources.reason: Approval review context overlaps with the existing FDA approval source and the staged DailyMed label without adding a new primary efficacy or safety result.

excluded_sources.title: Current and emerging therapeutic advancements for Niemann-Pick disease type C: where are we now and what does the future hold?
excluded_sources.reason: NPC therapeutics review context was screened but not staged because the primary OLE paper and label directly repair the public source gaps.

excluded_sources.reference: Zevra sponsor real-world, conference, and patent-listing leads
excluded_sources.reason: Sponsor and conference materials remain useful leads for later post-approval surveillance, but this repair did not find primary, independently citable real-world data that should change the public evidence map.

excluded_sources.title: Arimoclomol ALS and inclusion-body-myositis open-label extension registries
excluded_sources.reason: Both records are non-NPC disease-program extensions. They support boundary awareness but were not needed for the bounded NPC durability and label repair.

excluded_sources.title: Arimoclomol Gaucher and NPC early-access registries
excluded_sources.reason: Gaucher is wrong-scope disease context and the NPC early-access program has no posted results. Neither record changes the public outlook now.

excluded_sources.title: Safety and Efficacy of BGP-15 in Patients With Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
excluded_sources.reason: Terminated BGP-15 metabolic registry with no posted results. The published BGP-15 proof-of-concept paper remains the public source for this boundary.

excluded_sources.reference: Passive-heating, HSP90 imaging, HSP90 oncology, and generic heat-shock physiology registries
excluded_sources.reason: The same-day surveillance pass found no result-bearing chaperone-support intervention record in these searches. They remain taxonomy-boundary context rather than public evidence for the current track claim.

notes: The staged repair intentionally keeps the public evidence stage at human_functional_benefit and keeps confidence low.
The strongest added evidence is still disease-specific NPC evidence; it is not evidence that chaperone or heat-shock support broadly modifies aging biology.
Review and sponsor sources were screened for triangulation but not staged because primary sources directly repaired the high-priority gaps.
next_actions: Run source-fidelity, interpretation, and safety review before publishing the staged coverage-repair bundle.

If approved and published, return Chaperone and Heat-Shock Support to ordinary surveillance with special watch on post-approval Miplyffa safety, real-world NPC data, and non-NPC chaperone-support boundary evidence.
